claims.claim:
ABSTRACT
Generative ﬂow networks (GFlowNets), as an emerging technique, can be used as
an alternative to reinforcement learning for exploratory control tasks.

GFlowNet
aims to generate distribution proportional to the rewards over terminating states,
and to sample different candidates in an active learning fashion.

GFlowNets need
to form a DAG and compute the ﬂow matching loss by traversing the inﬂows and
outﬂows of each node in the trajectory.

No experiments have yet concluded that
GFlowNets can be used to handle continuous tasks.

In this paper, we propose gen-
erative continuous ﬂow networks (CFlowNets) that can be applied to continuous
control tasks.
First, we present the theoretical formulation of CFlowNets.

Then,
a training framework for CFlowNets is proposed, including the action selection
process, the ﬂow approximation algorithm, and the continuous ﬂow matching loss
function.

Afterward, we theoretically prove the error bound of the ﬂow approx-
imation.
